title: Map Ending
definition: The realization that a map, a representation of known territory, reaches its end when it encounters an unknown or uncharted entity.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Just like a map can only show us what's already been explored, there are some things that lie beyond the boundaries of our knowledge. These are the 'map-ending' moments, when we encounter something new.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The idea that the limits of our understanding — represented by maps — are reached when we encounter novel phenomena that cannot be accounted for within our existing frameworks.
difficulty_levels.expert: The concept of 'map-ending' signifies the boundary between known and unknown, a limit that challenges our understanding and forces us to redefine or expand our maps. These moments highlight the tentative nature of knowledge and the potential for discovery.

extra_fields.insight: Encountering the unknown challenges our existing maps and pushes us towards expansion and discovery.
